summary: Technology such as continuous glucose monitors does not by itself lead to improved glycaemic control; effective outcomes depend critically on ongoing patient education, lifestyle changes, and structured clinician involvement rather than technological tools alone. The core drivers of better blood sugar regulation are behavioral and clinical factors—not just digital devices.
